pattern: Backtracking with a single constraint: after placing '0', the next position must be '1', ensuring no two consecutive zeros.
hints: A valid binary string cannot contain '00' as a substring — after placing '0', the only valid next character is '1'.
Use backtracking: at each position always branch to '1', and branch to '0' only if the previous character was '1' (or it is the first position).
The total number of valid strings of length n equals the (n+2)-th Fibonacci number, so the output size stays manageable for n up to 18.

edge_inputs.description: n=2 — '00' is invalid, three strings remain
n=4 — verify '0000', '0010', '0001', '0100' etc. are filtered correctly

stress.description: n from 1 to 12 via size parameter
